what_it_does: A JSON allocation batch that enumerates six JSX source files from the claude-ds project, grouping them under an interpretive wave/batch path for ledger processing.
observations: All entries share project 'claude-ds' and root '/home/tim/company/design/claude-ds'.
The listed paths are JSX files in 'app/canvases/workshop/' and 'app/components/'.
There are no code symbols, content snippets, or checksums—only file references.
The batch mixes workshop canvases (Section, WidgetBuilder, WizardBuilder) with shared components (CanvasHeader, ChatRail, CommandPalette).
standouts: Workshop builder canvases are grouped alongside global UI components in the same batch.
Every entry uses the same project root, making this a homogeneous claude-ds slice.
The wave/batch naming implies a staged, ordered interpretive pipeline.
conventions: Uses a uniform tuple of project/path/root for each referenced file.
Stores the allocation as JSON in a hierarchical wave/alloc/batch file path.
Paths are repository-relative rather than absolute on the host.
concerns: Contains only file paths, with no version, checksum, or content hash to verify the referenced files.
No rationale is given for why these six files belong together in batch-008.
No timestamp or author metadata is recorded.
notes: A wave-002 allocation batch that lists six claude-ds JSX files for interpretive ledger processing.
questions: What criteria place these six files in batch-008 rather than another batch?
Does the order of the array carry semantic weight?
What output is expected from the interpretive wave for this batch?
purpose_vs_actual: The path suggests it should allocate source files to an interpretive wave/batch, and the content does exactly that by listing file references, so purpose matches actual.
intention_signals: It seems to be staging a curated slice of UI source files for downstream interpretation, fusion, or analysis within a larger code/design substrate ledger.
contribution: Provides a concrete, reusable manifest of claude-ds JSX files that a fused ledger can use as an input set for an interpretive batch.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON allocation batch file under wave-002 lists six JSX source files from the claude-ds project rooted at /home/tim/company/design/claude-ds.
intention_for_embedding: This file was meant to stage a specific set of canvas and component source files for interpretive ledger processing in wave-002.
